title: Tenant traffic ramp saturates one orders shard
summary: A large tenant (acme) starts a campaign that ramps its traffic ~5x. Its key range hashes onto orders-shard-3, which saturates (queue depth, latency) while shards 1 and 2 stay healthy. The service-wide aggregate looks only mildly degraded, hiding the hotspot. The cause is a traffic shift onto a partition, not a service regression.
question: Why did some orders requests get slow around 16:10 on 2026-06-14 while the service average looks fine?

ground_truth.notes: Analysis must be per-shard/per-tenant: shard-3 saturates while others are fine and the service aggregate is diluted. The disambiguator is the hot partition plus the tenant ramp, not a code change.
